exercises.prompt: « Mes grands-parents émigrèrent en 1950. » — which tense is « émigrèrent »?
exercises.explanation: -èrent is the classic passé simple ending of -er verbs: ils émigrèrent.

exercises.prompt: Where do you mainly meet the passé simple?

options.text: In novels, biographies and history writing

exercises.explanation: The passé simple is the literary narration tense. In speech, the passé composé does its job.

exercises.template: Dès qu'il {{b1}} terminé ses études, il partit.
exercises.explanation: In passé-simple narration, dès que takes the passé antérieur: dès qu'il eut terminé… il partit.

exercises.template: La maison qu'ils ont {{b1}} date de 1900.
exercises.explanation: The direct object (que = la maison) precedes the verb → agreement: achetée.

exercises.prompt: Complete: « Elle s'est ___ couper les cheveux. »
exercises.explanation: Faire + infinitive never agrees: elle s'est fait couper les cheveux.

exercises.prompt: The past before the past (one word).
exercises.template: Ils sont retournés au village où ils {{b1}} grandi.
exercises.explanation: Growing up happened before returning → plus-que-parfait: où ils avaient grandi.

exercises.prompt: Match each roots word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "The family settled in Lyon."
Listen to the family story. Why did the grandmother choose Lyon?
exercises.transcript: Ma grand-mère est née en Italie ; elle s'est installée à Lyon, où elle avait déjà de la famille.

options.text: She had found a job there

exercises.explanation: « où elle avait déjà de la famille » — the plus-que-parfait gives the pre-existing reason.

exercises.prompt: Read this biography extract (note the literary tenses) and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Marco Ferrari naquit en Toscane en 1932. À dix-huit ans, il quitta son pays natal et émigra vers la France. Dès qu'il eut trouvé un emploi aux usines de Lyon, il fit venir sa famille. Ses petits-enfants, qui ne parlaient pas un mot d'italien, redécouvrirent plus tard la langue que leur grand-père leur avait transmise en secret : les chansons.
exercises.passageEn: Marco Ferrari was born in Tuscany in 1932. At eighteen, he left his native country and emigrated to France. As soon as he had found a job in the Lyon factories, he sent for his family. His grandchildren, who didn't speak a word of Italian, later rediscovered the language their grandfather had passed on to them in secret: through songs.

questions.prompt: « Il naquit », « il quitta », « il fit venir » — these verbs are in the…
What had the grandfather secretly passed on?

exercises.explanation: Passé simple ↔ passé composé: same job, different register. Il alla (book) = il est allé (speech).

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Mes grands-parents émigrèrent en France en 1950.

options.text: My grandparents emigrated to France in 1950.
As soon as he had finished his studies, he left.
We settled in Lyon, where my ancestors had lived.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « Cette recette est un héritage familial. » — a heritage / inheritance.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “My grandparents emigrated to France in 1950.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Mes grands-parents émigrèrent en France en 1950.
exercises.targetEn: My grandparents emigrated to France in 1950.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Nous nous sommes installés à Lyon, où mes ancêtres avaient vécu.
exercises.targetEn: We settled in Lyon, where my ancestors had lived.
